api/Wolfgang.DbContextBuilderCore.DbCont.summary: Class DbContextBuilder<T> Uses the Builder pattern to create instances of DbContext types seeded with specified data. Inheritance object DbContextBuilder<T> Implements IDisposable Inherited Members object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: Wolfgang.DbContextBuilderCore Assembly: Wolfgang.DbContextBuilder-Core.dll Syntax public class DbContextBuilder<T> : IDisposable where T : DbContext Type Parameters Name Description T Remarks When using the SQLite provider, the builder holds an open SQLite in-memory connection. Dispose the builder only after all DbContext instances returned by BuildAsync() are no longer in use, as disposing the builder closes the shared connection and destroys the in-memory database. api/Wolfgang.DbContextBuilderCore.Sqlite.summary: Class SqliteForMsSqlServerModelCustomizer An implementation of SqliteModelCustomizer specifically configured to handle a DbContext designed for Microsoft SQL Server databases. Inheritance object ModelCustomizer SqliteModelCustomizer SqliteForMsSqlServerModelCustomizer Implements IModelCustomizer Inherited Members SqliteModelCustomizer.OverrideTableRenaming SqliteModelCustomizer.OverrideComputedValueHandling SqliteModelCustomizer.DefaultValueMap SqliteModelCustomizer.OverrideDefaultValueHandling SqliteModelCustomizer.Customize(ModelBuilder, DbContext) SqliteModelCustomizer.OverrideManyToManyTableHandling ModelCustomizer.Dependencies object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: Wolfgang.DbContextBuilderCore Assembly: Wolfgang.DbContextBuilder-Core.dll Syntax public class SqliteForMsSqlServerModelCustomizer : SqliteModelCustomizer, IModelCustomizer Remarks This class includes overrides to map common default value SQL functions from SQL Server to their SQLite equivalents. Constructors | Edit this page View Source SqliteForMsSqlServerModelCustomizer(ModelCustomizerDependencies) Creates a new instance of the SqliteForMsSqlServerModelCustomizer class. Declaration public SqliteForMsSqlServerModelCustomizer(ModelCustomizerDependencies dependencies) Parameters Type Name Description ModelCustomizerDependencies dependencies The dependencies for the model customizer. Remarks This class should not be created directly but rather added to the service collection by calling the UseSqliteForMsSqlServerModelCustomizer method on the DbContextBuilder class Implements IModelCustomizer

api/Wolfgang.DbContextBuilderCore.Sqlite.title: Class SqliteModelCustomizer | DbContextBuilder
api/Wolfgang.DbContextBuilderCore.Sqlite.summary: Class SqliteModelCustomizer Overrides the default model creation process in the DbContext{T} with configurations suitable for SQLite. Inheritance object ModelCustomizer SqliteModelCustomizer SqliteForMsSqlServerModelCustomizer Implements IModelCustomizer Inherited Members ModelCustomizer.Dependencies object.Equals(object) object.Equals(object, object) object.GetHashCode() object.GetType() object.MemberwiseClone() object.ReferenceEquals(object, object) object.ToString() Namespace: Wolfgang.DbContextBuilderCore Assembly: Wolfgang.DbContextBuilder-Core.dll Syntax public class SqliteModelCustomizer : ModelCustomizer, IModelCustomizer Remarks Unless the production database you are testing is also SQLite, there will be differences between your database and the context's configuration definition and SQLite's capabilities. This class provides some basic overrides to make your DbContext work in SQLite. This class provides some basic capabilities like, Renaming tables to avoid schema issues since SQLite does not support schemas. Removing computed values for columns since SQLite may not support the same functions. You can override the functionality provided in this class or if you will be frequently working in the same database engine, you can create your own ModelCustomizer, either from scratch or derived from this one, and override the desired functionality. Once you created you can reuse it over and over again. For example, you want to create a SqliteForOracleModelCustomizer that has overrides to make an DbContext created for Oracle work in SQLite. Constructors | Edit this page View Source SqliteModelCustomizer(ModelCustomizerDependencies) Initializes a new instance of the SqliteModelCustomizer class. Declaration public SqliteModelCustomizer(ModelCustomizerDependencies dependencies) Parameters Type Name Description ModelCustomizerDependencies dependencies The dependencies for the model customizer. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException dependencies is null. Properties | Edit this page View Source DefaultValueMap A dictionary where the key is the default value as defined in the model and the value is the value to replace it with. Declaration public IDictionary<string, string> DefaultValueMap { get; } Property Value Type Description IDictionary<string, string> Remarks Examples of a default values that may need to be replaced it getdate() and newid() which would be replaced with datetime('now') and lower(hex(randomblob(16))) respectively. | Edit this page View Source OverrideComputedValueHandling This method is called for each column that has a computed value defined in the model. Declaration public Func<string?, string?> OverrideComputedValueHandling { get; set; } Property Value Type Description Func<string, string> Remarks The default handling for this is to leave the computed value as is. However, Sqlite has limited support for computed values and the functions used in the model may be incompatible with SQLite. You can override this behavior by assigning a custom implementation to this property. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException | Edit this page View Source OverrideDefaultValueHandling This method is called for each column that has a default value defined in the model. Declaration public Func<string?, string?> OverrideDefaultValueHandling { get; set; } Property Value Type Description Func<string, string> Remarks The default implementation checks the DefaultValueMap dictionary to see if the existing default value is contained in the dictionary and if so, replaces it with the value in the dictionary. If the current default is not in the dictionary, it is left unchanged. You can override this behavior by assigning a custom implementation to this property Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException | Edit this page View Source OverrideManyToManyTableHandling This action is called for each entity type to handle many-to-many join table renaming. Declaration public Action<IMutableEntityType> OverrideManyToManyTableHandling { get; set; } Property Value Type Description Action<IMutableEntityType> Remarks The default implementation uses a heuristic to detect many-to-many join tables: an entity type with exactly two foreign keys and no navigations is assumed to be a join table. When detected, the table is renamed to "{LeftTable}_{RightTable}". You can override this behavior by assigning a custom implementation to this property. Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException | Edit this page View Source OverrideTableRenaming This method is called for each table in the database to rename the table since SQLite does not support schemas. Declaration public Func<(string? SchemaName, string TableName), string> OverrideTableRenaming { get; set; } Property Value Type Description Func<(string SchemaName, string TableName), string> Remarks The default implementation renames the table by prefixing the schema name to the table name. For example, a table named "Person" in schema Personnel would be renamed to "Personnel_Person". If the table doesn't have a schema "dbo" is used as the schema name. You can override this behavior by assigning a custom implementation to this property Exceptions Type Condition ArgumentNullException Methods | Edit this page View Source Customize(ModelBuilder, DbContext) Overrides the default model creation process in the DbContext{T} with configurations suitable for SQLite. Declaration public override void Customize(ModelBuilder modelBuilder, DbContext context) Parameters Type Name Description ModelBuilder modelBuilder The builder being used to construct the model. DbContext context The context instance that the model is being created for. Overrides ModelCustomizer.Customize(ModelBuilder, DbContext) Implements IModelCustomizer

index.html.title: DBContextBuilder | DbContextBuilder
index.html.summary: DBContextBuilder Uses the Builder pattern to create Entity Framework Core and classic DbContext instances using an in-memory database for testing purposes. With DbContextBuilder, you can easily set up a DbContext with predefined data, making it ideal for unit tests and integration tests without having to rely on an actual database whose data can be changed or deleted over time ReadMe Introduction Getting started API Quick Start Notes:
